description_long: This object is a stylized, blocky representation of an airplane, primarily rendered in shades of gray with a prominent yellow band or emblem at the front. The airplane features a broad, triangular wing structure that extends outward from a central fuselage, giving it a compact and angular appearance. The wings are integrated into the main body, with no visible separation, and the overall form is symmetrical from left to right. The front of the airplane is marked by a yellow square or rectangular detail, possibly representing a cockpit window or nose cone. The underside shows small, dark protrusions that may represent landing gear or support struts. The surface is uniformly matte and lacks fine detailing, emphasizing a geometric, voxel-like aesthetic.
description: A blocky, gray airplane with a triangular wing shape and a yellow front emblem, featuring simple geometric lines and minimal surface detail.
description_view_0: Front view showing the yellow square detail at the nose and the full width of the triangular wings.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the stepped, layered structure of the fuselage and the yellow accent at the front.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the symmetrical, angular wing layout and the absence of visible tail or engine details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring view 1, with the yellow accent visible and the blocky wing structure apparent.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight, triangular planform with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_tail_design: No distinct tail structure visible; appears to lack a conventional tail assembly.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed, with small dark protrusions suggesting simple landing gear units.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, indicated by the yellow square at the nose; appears enclosed with no visible canopy or windows.
